skill.name: visual-design-foundations
skill.description: Apply typography, color, spacing, and iconography principles to build accessible visual systems and design tokens.
skill.summary: Build cohesive visual systems with typography, color, spacing, icons, and accessible design tokens.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Reviewed the 144 static detections and found no confirmed security issues. The alerts are false positives from Markdown code fences, JavaScript regex .exec usage, design token names, CSS font metric properties, SVG namespace text, and normal documentation links. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, autonomous command execution, or malicious network behavior.

content.value_statement: Inconsistent typography, colors, spacing, and icons make interfaces harder to use and maintain. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code define practical visual systems with accessible tokens and reusable patterns.
actual_capabilities: Defines typography scales with font sizes, line heights, weights, and responsive type guidance.
Creates semantic color token structures, dark mode strategies, and contrast checking guidance.
Documents spacing systems based on grids, component gaps, layout rhythm, and sizing scales.
Specifies icon sizing, SVG usage patterns, icon button states, and sprite generation concepts.
Provides Tailwind, CSS, and React examples for implementing reusable visual design foundations.
limitations: Does not generate finished brand identities or original artwork by itself.
Requires project context to choose final fonts, colors, and component proportions.
Does not replace manual accessibility testing with real content and states.
Examples are guidance snippets and may need adaptation for a specific framework.

use_cases.title: Create a New Design Token Set
use_cases.description: Define practical typography, spacing, color, and icon tokens before building a new interface.

use_cases.title: Improve Interface Consistency
use_cases.description: Audit existing screens for visual hierarchy, spacing drift, color misuse, and inconsistent icon treatment.

use_cases.title: Document a Lightweight Style Guide
use_cases.description: Turn design choices into clear usage rules that developers can apply across components.

prompt_templates.title: Review Visual Basics
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit of an existing screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this interface for typography, color contrast, spacing, and icon consistency. Give prioritized fixes with simple design-token suggestions.

prompt_templates.title: Define Starter Tokens
prompt_templates.scenario: Create foundations for a small product
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a starter visual design system for a SaaS dashboard. Include typography scale, color tokens, spacing scale, icon sizes, and usage rules.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt Tokens to Tailwind
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert design decisions into implementation guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate these brand colors and type choices into Tailwind-ready design tokens. Include accessibility notes and dark mode considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Design System
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this component library for visual system gaps. Identify token drift, hierarchy problems, contrast risks, icon inconsistencies, and migration steps.

output_examples.input: A team needs a design foundation for a new analytics dashboard.
output: A concise typography scale for headings, body text, labels, and data-heavy tables.
Semantic color roles for brand, success, warning, error, surfaces, borders, and text.
Spacing and icon rules that keep dense dashboard layouts readable and consistent.

output_examples.input: An existing product has inconsistent spacing and unclear hierarchy.
output: A prioritized audit of spacing drift, weak contrast, font overload, and missing states.
Recommended token names and replacement values for repeated layout patterns.
A short remediation plan for components with the highest user impact.

output_examples.input: A designer wants dark mode guidance for existing tokens.
output: A semantic token model that separates brand, surface, text, border, and state colors.
Contrast checks to verify readable text and interactive states in both themes.
Guidance for avoiding direct color names in component-level decisions.

best_practices: Start with semantic tokens before adding component-specific exceptions.
Check contrast, touch targets, and state colors with real content early.
Document usage rules for typography, spacing, colors, and icons alongside token values.
anti_patterns: Using arbitrary one-off values when an existing spacing or type token fits.
Naming tokens by visual appearance when their purpose may change across themes.
Adding many font families, weights, or icon styles without a clear hierarchy need.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create visual design foundations for typography, color, spacing, iconography, and design tokens.

faq.question: Can it help with accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for contrast, readable type, touch targets, and visual hierarchy.

faq.question: Does it work with Tailwind CSS?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes Tailwind-oriented token examples and can adapt guidance to Tailwind projects.

faq.question: Is this only for designers?
faq.answer: No. Designers, frontend engineers, and design system teams can use it to align visual decisions.

faq.question: Will it choose final brand colors for me?
faq.answer: It can suggest accessible structures and palettes, but final brand decisions need project context.

faq.question: Can it audit an existing interface?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide screenshots, components, or token files, and ask for a visual consistency review.
